title: VRChat

notes:
The devs announced that they would be adding EAC with only 24 hours notice. Everyone we know who used VRChat was appalled, as this would kill client-side mods that provide basic accessibility features, significant Quality-of-Life improvements, and in some cases allow the game to run at all. It was also pointed out that the EAC implementation wasn't even effective for the stated reasons, and was completely bypassed within hours of the announcement.

Despite all this, including the most-upvoted post ever on their own feedback forum, the devs pushed the update to stable anyway.

Our full obituary: https://steamcommunity.com/id/gameon123/recommended/438100?snr=1_5_9__402
title: VRChat

notes: EAC added but works, performance issues as usual for this game
title: VRChat

notes:
I am deeply disappointed and distraught that the VRChat team has chosen not to listen to its community and to go ahead with this poorly-received update.

If you are a VRC+ subscriber, I urge you to join the boycott against VRChat and cancel your subscription. Seeing their only revenue figures drop may be the only way to get through the thick skulls of VRChat management and investors.



VRChat is also lying in their blog post when they say this:
> **Does this prevent people from playing VRChat on [..] Linux via Proton?**
>
> **No**, we’ve tested this! EAC works fine on these platforms.
title: VRChat

notes: Everything up to loading a world works, including logging in. It has been borked after the update and the developers ban or delete comments of anyone pointing out the issues with the Steam Deck. I do not believe that this game should be Steam Deck verified any longer.
title: VRChat

notes:
VRChat team claims that EAC doesn't affect Linux users.
This is only true in Desktop mode. 
As far as I know, no one has got the game working in VR on Linux after the EAC.
In previous versions the game used to work fine with Valve Index, expect h264 video player decoding.
VRChat hasn't made any updated statement yet, but they have marked related bug thread as "Tracked internally"
https://feedback.vrchat.com/open-beta/p/1212-unable-to-launch-eac-vrchat-in-vr-on-linux

I haven't found any workarounds at the time of writing. You have to stick with Desktop mode until this is fixed.
title: VRChat

I am using an unmodded version of VRChat, and this error message did not appear until after the Easy Anti-Cheat update. From here VRChat only allows me to quit the program, effectively making it impossible to play.

notes: Launches to EAC. After the EAC launcher closes the game does not start, and steam says the game is Running. VRChat.exe is open in the background, and uses 0% CPU and about 200-300MBs of RAM. This seems to be an issue with many games that use newer EAC (start_protected_game), such as Deceit and MultiVersus.
